themes.exampleQuote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
Add that delicious waffle to it and you go into a brand new realm of delicious.
The owner is extremely friendly and nice and helped make the experience that much better.
The comment along the lines of 'I'm not going to waste any more ice cream on you all,' delivered in a rude tone, made us feel uncomfortable.
The sage salted almond on a waffle was so good!

notable_quote: I'm an ice cream snob and this is some of the best ice cream I've ever had.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers are emphatic about the product, the Liège waffle format and unusual flavors like sage salted almond earn repeated superlatives, but a recurring friction around sampling limits and staff tone has surfaced in at least two reviews with enough specificity to be taken seriously.
quarter_narrative: Treat entered Spring 2026 with a 623-review foundation and a 98% five-star hold that most Shadyside shops would envy, and its visual catalog, 202 photos spread across a full Google category set, keeps the listing competitive in search. What it didn't do was post to Instagram: zero posts, zero reels, and 196 days of silence from an account with a 7.65% engagement rate that suggests a genuinely warm audience waiting to be used. That gap is the story, because the Pittsburgh Sweets category is being paced right now by Page's, whose defining signal this issue is social momentum, the exact axis where Treat has gone completely dark. Moving from Ones to Watch requires more than a stable rating; it requires the city to keep encountering the business in new contexts.
tldr_read: 623 reviews at 98% five-star and a maxed-out Google presence, but the Instagram account has been silent for 196 days, Ones to Watch, #3 in Pittsburgh Ice Cream Shops.
tldr_meaning: Every signal axis but one is at or near the family median; the Instagram dormancy is the single variable separating Treat's current rank from a legitimate push toward the top of the category.

playbook.headline: Restart the Instagram account immediately
playbook.action: 196 days of silence on a 7.65%-engagement account is a direct cost, post the waffle and a named flavor within this week.

playbook.headline: Post a visible sample policy at the counter
playbook.action: Two reviews cite the same staff friction around sample limits; a simple posted guideline removes ambiguity and protects the 98% rating.

playbook.headline: Tag specific flavors in photo uploads
playbook.action: The visual catalog is strong at 202 photos, but named flavors like sage salted almond give search and creator context that generic food shots don't.
